pattern: A subarray's length is divisible by k exactly when its two prefix-sum endpoints share the same index remainder mod k, so track the best prefix sum seen at each remainder.
hints: Express any subarray's sum as a difference of two prefix sums, and notice its length is just the difference of their indices.
That length is divisible by k exactly when the two prefix indices have the same remainder mod k -- so group prefix sums by remainder.
Scan once left to right, and for each index's remainder subtract the smallest prefix sum seen so far at that same remainder before updating the running minimum.

edge_inputs.description: k equal to one, every subarray qualifies
k equal to the array length, only the whole array qualifies
all negative values, the least negative qualifying subarray still wins
a short qualifying subarray beats a longer one with a worse total
smallest possible array with k equal to its own length
the best qualifying subarray must skip a large negative dip in the middle
all zeros with k a proper divisor of the length

stress.description: a long array with a random divisor k
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(200000, size if size >= 1 else 1))
    nums = [rng.randint(-1000000000, 1000000000) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [nums, k]
